I build production AI systems and prove they work

Applied AI with an educator's edge. I design, ship, and validate real systems: agentic pipelines, payment integrity, AI authoring tools. Then I make them usable.

Featured Project
Applied AI · Eval-driven

Eval-driven AI authoring

Each authoring concern maps to the right primitive, with a hard gate that refuses to let an LLM grade its own work.

Selected work

Production AI systems, hardened software, and the systems thinking that connects them.

Stripe billing, productionized

A stale customer ID silently double-charged a card $138, so I made a test-mode billing system safe to take real money: a 95-case test harness from zero, a self-healing webhook resilience layer, and the live migration.

Stripe Payments Webhooks Idempotency
View case study

Voice-to-Prototype agentic pipeline

A GitHub-native agentic pipeline: a labelled issue triggers a constrained Claude Code run that opens a draft PR plus preview. The workflow owns git, not the model. Trust-gated and injection-hardened. I debugged it across a live 4-PR arc, then honestly held it behind a pivot.

Agent development GitHub Actions Prompt-injection hygiene Claude Code
View case study

Second Brain: LLM-maintained wiki

A compounding personal wiki where the LLM compiles knowledge once and keeps it current. It summarises, cross-references, and flags contradictions across 100 interlinked pages (0 hand-written) from 66 sources. A deliberate alternative to query-time RAG.

Knowledge engineering RAG alternative CLAUDE.md Obsidian
View case study

Firestore security rules + emulator suite

Threat-modelled the economic attack surface of a SaaS data layer (no self-granting a paid tier, no inflating credits) and defended it with field allowlisting and diff-based update guards. Proven with 59 allow/deny cases against the shipped rules in the Firebase emulator.

Firestore Security Emulator testing Threat modeling
View case study

Natural-language control for Ableton Live

Integrated an open-source MCP bridge to drive a live Ableton session by voice or text. The real work wasn't building the bridge. It was a one-command idempotent install (7 steps to 1) and a log-driven diagnosis.

MCP Systems integration macOS Debugging
View case study

Content drift detection (agentic)

A three-skill agent pipeline (release-analyzer → content-mapper → change-generator) that detects which docs an API release impacts and drafts the updates. A human reviews them before they reach developers.

Agent skills Progressive disclosure Drift detection Human-in-the-loop
View case study

AI grading & evaluation system

An LLM evaluation system that scores submissions against a structured rubric, with quality gates that catch ungrounded or hallucinated judgments. It runs at a fraction of the cost and time of manual assessment.

Claude API Evals Quality gates Python
View case study

Geocoding accuracy campaign

A diagnose-and-fix campaign on ~110K planning permits. I reframed a 5.8% per-run geocoding rate as a data-quality problem and wrote the address normalizer that recovered 47% of the failing backlog, lifting whole-dataset coverage to 98.3%.

Data quality Geocoding Address normalization Debugging
View case study

Single-source-of-truth docs program

A 5-person program that killed the cross-microsite duplication degrading search and RAG retrieval. One canonical source per concept, codified as a reusable 0-4 classification skill. ~2,660 lines consolidated across 8 domains, 1 microsite sunset.

Information architecture RAG retrieval Single source of truth
View case study

In-app bug-reporting pipeline

A five-stage serverless pipeline (scoped screenshot upload, private GitHub issue, HMAC-verified resolution webhook, two-pass retention GC) shipped to all users with distributed-systems hygiene and a ~1.3:1 test-to-source ratio.

Serverless HMAC Idempotency PII handling
View case study

Distributed scraper CI orchestration

The CI orchestration that keeps a brittle ~40-job scraping fleet reliable: matrix failure isolation, an LFS-safe concurrent-commit loop that survives main moving mid-run, and an infra-vs-bug failure taxonomy with bounded auto-rerun.

GitHub Actions Git LFS Reliability CI/CD
View case study

Alex Chen: synthetic eval persona

A synthetic user that validates documentation by simulating a senior developer's feedback patterns. An evaluation persona for catching gaps before real developers hit them.

Synthetic users Prompt engineering Evals
View case study

B2B Commerce learning path

Owned a B2B modeling path end to end. SME-validated, authored for the Functional Architect persona, with every API example verified against the RAML source. Module 2 passed the eval-driven critique gate at 88/100.

Curriculum architecture SME synthesis API-verified Built & shipped
View case study

Modular learning path ecosystem

Scalable content architecture supporting 9 paths, 30+ modules, and 200+ pages of technical material. One information architecture serving thousands of developers.

Information architecture Markdown Git
View case study

Developer Essentials learning path

A 20-hour curriculum that takes enterprise developers from zero to production-ready through hands-on, API-first mastery.

Curriculum design TypeScript Java API education
View case study

Marcus Aurelius AI mentor

A conversational AI companion built on Socratic, persona-driven dialogue. An experiment in how far a single well-designed prompt system can carry an experience.

Conversational AI Prompt engineering Next.js
View case study

3 Steps Away: social-justice education platform

Co-founded an education enterprise teaching high schoolers to respond to complex social issues through visual storytelling and experiential learning. Where the systems thinking started.

Founder Experiential learning Youth education
View case study

How I build

I don't just call models. I build with the full agentic toolchain. What sets the work apart is fluency operating the stack, not just the API.

Subagents Multi-agent orchestration Dynamic workflows Routines (scheduled agents) Loops Eval harnesses Plan mode Agentic loops Worktrees Headless / CLI in CI MCP server development Skills CLAUDE.md schema design CLI in VS Code

A builder whose years as an educator make the systems land

I design, build, and ship production AI systems: agentic CI pipelines, payment-integrity infrastructure, AI authoring architectures. Then I make them legible to the people and models that depend on them. Building something well and explaining it clearly don't always come from the same person, and I work at both.

As a technical co-founder at storipro, I shipped the production billing layer, hardened the data-security model, and built an autonomous issue-to-PR pipeline. At commercetools, I architected an orchestrator-agent authoring system with an eval harness and drove a single-source-of-truth documentation program across the platform. On my own time I keep building. An LLM-maintained knowledge base, an MCP control layer for Ableton Live.

I came up as an educator, a decade of it across three continents, and that's exactly why my systems land. The real craft of the classroom is earning enough trust that people actually engage. I build the same way, for the people and models that have to use the output. That's the edge, not the lane I'm leaving.

Read my full story

Let's work together

If you're building applied-AI systems, or you need someone who can architect them, ship them, and make them land, I'd love to connect.

Email me Connect on LinkedIn

Luke Panaccio

I build production AI systems and make them usable. Years as an educator taught me to build for the person on the other end, so the systems actually get used.
